userRequest:
$babysitter:yolo  You are an agent version tracker. Check for new releases of the ORIGINAL upstream agent CLI products — NOT our babysitter plugin packages.

STEP 1: Build atlas and agent-catalog.
```
npm run build --workspace=@a5c-ai/atlas
npm run build --workspace=@a5c-ai/agent-catalog
```

STEP 2: Get the target list using the SAME logic as `scripts/sync-external-plugin-repos.mjs` uses in `buildTargetsFromCatalog()`. Read that file to understand the pattern, then run the provided listPluginTargetDescriptors command. Also check adapter source for additional install commands with grep. Merge both lists. EXCLUDE all @a5c-ai/* packages.

STEP 3: For EACH upstream agent, check the latest published version. `npm view <package>@latest version` for npm packages. Search the web for agents distributed differently (pip, binary, etc.).

STEP 4: Compare against AgentVersion records in the graph. Read files in `packages/atlas/graph/agent-stack/agent-versions/` and `packages/atlas/graph/agent-stack/products/` to see what versions are already recorded.

STEP 5: For new versions not in the graph, research release notes/changelog/blog posts, create/update AgentVersion YAML, and note capability changes, new CLI flags, breaking changes, transport/API changes.

STEP 6: If graph files changed, rebuild atlas, branch `agent-versions/daily-$(date +%Y-%m-%d)`, commit all changes, create one PR against staging.

STEP 7: For each new agent+version pair, check existing issues with label `agent-version-update` and search `<agent> <version>`. If none exists, create an issue with labels `agent-version-update,graph-update` and a body containing actual research, specific affected system surfaces, install/migration notes, and a specific assimilation checklist.

STEP 8: Print a summary table with ALL agents checked.

IMPORTANT: Cover every upstream agent systematically. Do not stop after one. One PR for all changes in this run.
